sanskrit:
ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते।
ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम्॥
transliteration: jyotiṣām api taj jyotis tamasaḥ param ucyate | jñānaṃ jñeyaṃ jñāna-gamyaṃ hṛdi sarvasya viṣṭhitam ||
literal_meaning: Among lights, That is the Light; beyond darkness it is called. Knowledge, the knowable, approached by knowledge, in the heart of all it is seated.
contextual_meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that the true Self is the inner light that outshines every external source, the silent knower beneath thoughts, always present in the heart.
psychological_state: A moment of luminous recognition: the felt-sense that the anxious narrator in the head is not the deepest self.
trigger_scenario: Lying in bed with racing thoughts, you place a hand on your chest, feel the heartbeat, and suddenly realize: 'There’s a quiet place behind the noise that’s been here all along.'
anchor_text: The light within you outshines every star.
